Joseph Murphy, who is combat
ting the 100000 issue of park
bonds voted last May, has taken
the case into court through his at
torneys. Birdseye & Smith. He
has not asked for temporary re-
straining order. as the corporation
counsel has agreed that the bonds
shall not be issued until the out
come of the suit is determined.


The complaint alleges that the
election by which the bond issue
was decided was not legal for the
following reasons:


That the city charter did not
give power for the election as
held; that tie election officials
were not properly sworn and quail
fled and that number of the
Judges were appointed according tC
the wishes of citizens who paid fol
the services, instead of having i'm
partial election officials.
